en: house
cat
dog
good morning
thank you

to bounce back

Examples:
He bounced back quickly.
The team bounced back after the loss.

to downplay / to minimize

downplay — make something seem less important
minimize — reduce or underestimate something

to judge / to condemn

judge — criticize or form an opinion
condemn — strongly criticize

to devalue / to undervalue / to diminish / to invalidate

Examples:
Don’t devalue me.
Don’t diminish me.
Don’t invalidate my feelings.
He constantly undervalues my efforts.
attraction — repulsion
contraction — expansion
almost done / almost finished / nearly there

to get closure / to find closure

Examples:
I need to get closure.
She finally found closure.

to finish something unresolved / to put something behind me / to wrap things up

Examples:
I want to finish something unresolved.
I just want to put it behind me.
Let’s wrap things up.
safety / security
to be safe / to feel safe
to worry too much about one’s safety / to be overly concerned about one’s safety / to be too focused on staying safe

distinctly

Example:
I distinctly remember closing the door.

ru: после того, как прошёл или перенёс что-то: операцию, лечение, обследование, стресс
en:
after undergoing

Meaning:
Use it when someone has gone through a process, usually something medical, difficult, or formal.

Examples:
After undergoing surgery.
After undergoing examination.
After undergoing treatment.

ru: уменьшительно-ласкательная форма слова или имени; ласковое обращение
en:
diminutive / diminutive form / affectionate diminutive

Meaning:
A shorter or cuter form of a word or name; for affectionate names, use pet name or term of endearment.

Examples:
"Kitty" is a diminutive of "cat".
A diminutive form of a name.
"Sweetie", "baby", and "honey" are affectionate pet names.
